steps.title: Apply the no-solutions condition
steps.content:
Expand and rearrange $9x + 5 = a(x+b)$:
$$9x + 5 = ax + ab$$
$$(9-a)x = ab - 5$$
A linear equation $cx = d$ has no solutions when $c = 0$ and $d eq 0$ (creating the contradiction $0 = d$).

For this equation to have no solutions:
  • $9 - a = 0 \Rightarrow a = 9$
  • $ab - 5 eq 0 \Rightarrow 9b - 5 eq 0 \Rightarrow b eq rac{5}{9}$

steps.title: Evaluate which statements must be true
steps.content:
I. $a = 9$: Must be true. ✓
II. $b = 5$: Not required; we only need $b \neq \frac{5}{9}$. Values like $b = 1, 2, 3$ also work. ✗
III. $b \neq \frac{5}{9}$: Must be true. ✓

Statements I and III must be true. That matches choice D — D is correct.
